channel.title: Convergence Daily
channel.description: Real Convergence Daily episodes published through the dual-native engine.

item.title: The Camera They Locked in a Drawer
item.description: The company most likely to see the future first is the one with the most to lose from it.

item.title: The Box Nobody Priced
item.description: The Box Nobody Priced Thesis The thing that reprices the world never looks like a breakthrough. Signals sig_1: sector_corpus sig_2: decision_packets sig_3: options_scanner sig_4: mispricing_book sig_5: peptides sig_6: cicadas sig_7: synthetic-biology What would falsify it Reject the setup if packet invalidations fire, scanner asymmetry disappears, or the book exposure already prices the obvious expression.

item.title: The Future They Gave Away
item.description: The Future They Gave Away Thesis Inventing the future and capturing it are two completely different skills, and almost no one has both. Signals sig_1: sector_corpus sig_2: decision_packets sig_3: options_scanner sig_4: mispricing_book sig_5: ai-infrastructure sig_6: cicadas sig_7: synthetic-biology What would falsify it Reject the setup if packet invalidations fire, scanner asymmetry disappears, or the book exposure already prices the obvious

item.title: defense AI: The Scanner Versus the Corpus
item.description: defense AI: The Scanner Versus the Corpus Thesis The sector corpus now has 1 decision packets and 1 theses pointing at defense AI, cicadas, and synthetic biology, while the scanner highlights PLTR and the paper book already tracks 6 open and 62 closed rows. Signals sig_1: sector_corpus sig_2: decision_packets sig_3: options_scanner sig_4: mispricing_book sig_5: defense-ai sig_6: cicadas sig_7: synthetic-biology What

item.title: AI infrastructure: The Scanner Versus the Corpus
item.description: The sector corpus now has 1 decision packets and 1 theses pointing at AI infrastructure, cicadas, and synthetic biology, while the scanner highlights AVGO and TSM and the paper book already tracks 6 open and 62 closed rows.

item.title: Everyone Priced the Drug; No One Priced the Factory
item.description: Everyone Priced the Drug; No One Priced the Factory Thesis The GLP-1 options market has converged on deep out-of-the-money originator calls at peak hype pricing while the picks-and-shovels CDMO manufacturing layer — where the corpus locates the durable structural exposure — has no US derivatives expression; the decision packet verdict is watchlist_candidate because the expression vehicle does not yet exist

item.title: The Robot Has Left the Lab; the Market Has Not
item.description: The Robot Has Left the Lab; the Market Has Not Thesis Humanoid robotics has moved from demos into commercial pilots at BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Tesla, Symbotic, and Serve Robotics, but the options scanner returns one deep-OTM TSLA contract at MEDIUM convergence — the corpus is ahead of market pricing, the revaluation catalyst is Figure AI's financing or IPO timing, and that

item.title: bitcoin mining: The Scanner Versus the Corpus
item.description: bitcoin mining: The Scanner Versus the Corpus Thesis The sector corpus now has 1 decision packets and 1 theses pointing at bitcoin mining, cicadas, and synthetic biology, while the scanner highlights MARA and the paper book already tracks 7 open and 59 closed rows. Signals sig_1: sector_corpus sig_2: decision_packets sig_3: options_scanner sig_4: mispricing_book sig_5: bitcoin-mining sig_6: cicadas sig_7: synthetic-biology What

Signals sig_1: sector_corpus sig_2: decision_packets sig_3: options_scanner sig_4: mispricing_book sig_5: ai-infrastructure sig_6: cicadas sig_7:

item.title: peptides: The Scanner Versus the Corpus
item.description: peptides: The Scanner Versus the Corpus Thesis The sector corpus now has 1 decision packets and 1 theses pointing at peptides, cicadas, and synthetic biology, while the scanner highlights LLY and NVO and the paper book already tracks 7 open and 59 closed rows. Signals sig_1: sector_corpus sig_2: decision_packets sig_3: options_scanner sig_4: mispricing_book sig_5: peptides sig_6: cicadas sig_7: synthetic-biology What

item.title: The Factory the Chip Trade Forgot
item.description: The Factory the Chip Trade Forgot Thesis The options market has absorbed AI infrastructure through NVIDIA at peak-hype convergence while leaving TSMC — the CoWoS packaging node where BIS export-control compounding and HBM supply constraints converge — at minimum scanner attention, with the sector's strongest-sourced corpus claim on the primary risk variable and convergence at 0.25 on the equity. Signals

item.title: All the Bottlenecks, None of the Asymmetry
item.description: AI infrastructure is the loudest thesis in public markets and the corpus confirms the underlying bottleneck dynamics are real — HBM and advanced packaging constraint, China export-control compounding risk, and hyperscaler capex pull-through visible in secondary-layer vendor IR — but the options scanner shows peak-hype convergence at the headline names and no clean asymmetric expression in the secondary bottleneck-stack names where the information edge actually lives.

item.title: The Clocks Are Not Running at the Same Speed
item.description: The Clocks Are Not Running at the Same Speed Thesis Bitcoin mining has split into two structurally distinct businesses with different risk clocks — pure-play hashprice names (MARA, RIOT, CLSK) cooling post-peak and AI/HPC infrastructure platforms (CORZ, HUT, IREN, BTBT, HIVE) building lease duration quietly — while the corpus carries 0.80–0.83 confidence on three independent claims and the decision packet

item.title: When the Shortage List Gets Resolved
item.description: DTC telehealth platforms have built material compounded GLP-1 revenues explicitly contingent on FDA shortage-list status — a binary regulatory event that collapses this revenue with no appeal mechanism.

item.title: Petahash Was the Cover Story
item.description: Petahash Was the Cover Story Thesis U.S. public bitcoin mining has split into two structurally distinct businesses — pure-play hashprice beta (MARA, RIOT, CLSK) and AI/HPC power-infrastructure platforms (CORZ, HUT, IREN, BTBT, HIVE, WULF, BITF, CIFR) — at 0.82 corpus confidence sourced from Q1-Q2 2026 public miner filings, while the options scanner covers only the hashprice half at post_peak convergence

item.title: Five Hundred Patients Before the Market Noticed
item.description: Five Hundred Patients Before the Market Noticed Thesis CRISPR Therapeutics has cleared the first commercial-compounding threshold in public gene editing — $43M Q1 2026 Casgevy revenue, 500-plus patient initiations, $2.44B cash — while the scanner returns one passing contract across the entire synthetic biology theme and equities have not re-rated from clinical-stage to commercial-compounder multiples. Simultaneously, the 2026-2027 engineered-biology cohort

item.title: The Factory Behind the Drug Nobody Can Get
item.description: The Factory Behind the Drug Nobody Can Get Thesis Peptide API manufacturing capacity is the structural constraint on GLP-1 supply — observable in primary CDMO investor filings at 0.80 corpus confidence — while originator names trade at peak-hype convergence and the manufacturing layer sits entirely outside option flow. The picks-and-shovels CDMO expression carries lower franchise-headline risk and has not been

item.title: The Gearbox Between Demo and Revenue
item.description: Humanoid robotics has crossed from demo to commercial pilot — Tesla/Optimus in quarterly IR, Figure/BMW under formal agreement, Apptronik at Mercedes-Benz — while the options market prices TSLA at MEDIUM/growing convergence (0.50) with no isolation of the actuator supply constraint that governs cost-per-unit and deployment velocity.

item.title: The Market Is Long the Brand, Short the Molecule
item.description: The Market Is Long the Brand, Short the Molecule Thesis The GLP-1 obesity trade is fully priced at the originator level — LLY and NVO sit at peak-hype convergence with elevated IV and deep-OTM options expressing a narrative the market already knows. The corpus thesis is that the structurally durable exposure is upstream: specialized peptide-API CDMO capacity, where the supply

item.title: The Optical Layer Running Ahead of the Tape
item.description: The Optical Layer Running Ahead of the Tape Thesis Photonic computing has cleared three simultaneous conditions for a quiet setup: NVIDIA-anchored revenue inflection at 0.88 corpus confidence, private chiplet validation at multi-billion-dollar marks, and zero passing options contracts on LITE or COHR. The scanner's loudest signals sit in peak-hype names where the narrative has fully taxed the trade. The cross-sector

item.title: Revenue Is Moving in the Dark
item.description: Revenue Is Moving in the Dark Thesis Photonic computing has crossed a confirmed revenue inflection — Lumentum +90% YoY, Coherent +21% YoY, NVIDIA $4B structural anchor — while the scanner records zero options contracts on both primary names and the convergence scorer still classifies the theme as emerging; the same AI-capex supercycle is visible in nuclear-SMR and defense-AI, but options

item.title: The Light Nobody Is Trading
item.description: The Light Nobody Is Trading Thesis Silicon photonics is generating the real revenue inflection inside AI infrastructure while options flow concentrates in peak-hype names one layer up the stack. NVIDIA's $4B anchor commitment in March 2026, combined with 90% and 21% year-over-year revenue growth at Lumentum and Coherent respectively, and a validated private chiplet layer (Lightmatter, Ayar Labs, Marvell-Celestial), constitutes

item.title: The Bandwidth Wall Is Already Photons
item.description: The Bandwidth Wall Is Already Photons Thesis Optical interconnect has crossed from research to production revenue inside the hyperscaler AI buildout, anchored by NVIDIA's March 2026 $4B dual commitment to Lumentum and Coherent, yet the options scanner finds zero qualifying structures for either name while private chiplet marks at Lightmatter ($4.4B) and Ayar Labs ($3.75B) signal the same 12-to-24-month private-leads-public
